The ELM implementation has two value mismatches with the CPG requirements:

The ELM implementation has an age threshold mismatch. The CPG requires an age range of 30-85 years, but the ELM implementation uses an age range of 30-80 years, with the upper limit being 5 years less than specified.

The ELM implementation has a mismatch in the "Time Intervals (Lookback Periods)" section. The CPG requirements do not specify a lookback period of "9 g%" for the HbA1c Laboratory Test, whereas the ELM implementation does. Additionally, the unit "g%" is unclear in this context, as HbA1c is typically measured as a percentage (%). The CPG requires the most recent HbA1c result, but does not specify a time interval. The ELM implementation should be revised to match the CPG requirements.
The ELM implementation has a lookback period of 6 months, but the CPG requires a lookback period of 1 year for anxiety disorder screening.

The age threshold in the ELM implementation (Age >= 30 years) does not match the age threshold in the CPG requirements (Age >= 15 years AND Age <= 65 years). The ELM implementation should include both the lower (15 years) and upper (65 years) bounds.

The ELM implementation has a mismatch in the age thresholds. The CPG requires an age range of 21 to 65 years, but the ELM implementation only mentions age thresholds of 30 years and 65 years, missing the lower threshold of 21 years.

The ELM implementation has a lookback period of 3 months for recent depression screenings, whereas the CPG requires a lookback period of 1 year. The age threshold in the ELM implementation is not explicitly mentioned as being compared to the CPG's requirement of age >= 12 years, but assuming it matches the "in: In Demographic" description, it still does not address the time interval mismatch.

The Age Threshold in the ELM implementation (Age >= 25 years) does not match the Age Threshold in the CPG requirements (Age >= 18 years).

The ELM implementation has a lookback period of 6 months for recent DEXA scans, but the CPG requirements specify a lookback period of 2 years. This is a mismatch between the ELM implementation and the CPG requirements.
The ELM implementation has an age threshold of 55 years, but the CPG requirement specifies an age threshold of 65 years. The correct age threshold should be Age >= 65 years and Age <= 75 years.

The ELM implementation does not explicitly mention the 20 pack-year smoking history and the quit time within the past 15 years, which are required in the CPG.
